title: TC001-Generate a content matrix successfully and replace the sample matrix
description: Verifies a user can fill all required intake fields, generate a matrix, and see the sample banner disappear after a successful response.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Fill the Product Name field with 'Acme Launch Kit' (index 2) as the immediate action.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Acme Launch Kit')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('A toolkit that helps startups plan, produce, and schedule launch content across channels.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Early-stage founders and small marketing teams')
        
        # -> Fill Core Features (index 5) with 'Content calendar templates, idea generator, channel guidelines', then click the Generate Content Matrix button (index 70). After clicking, verify loading skeletons appear and then the generated matrix replaces the sample banner.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[4]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Content calendar templates, idea generator, channel guidelines')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC006-Generate matrix and verify it restores on reload without sample banner
description: Verifies a successfully generated matrix is persisted and restored on a subsequent page load with no sample banner and expected content visible.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type the provided product info into the four fields and click the Generate Content Matrix button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Acme Analytics')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('A B2B analytics platform that turns product usage into actionable insights.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Product managers, growth teams, and data analysts')
        
        # -> Type 'Funnels, cohort analysis, dashboards, alerts' into the Core Features field, click Generate Content Matrix, wait for generation, then reload the root page to verify the generated matrix persists (no sample banner) and Tier 1 content is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[4]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Funnels, cohort analysis, dashboards, alerts')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Reload the root page (navigate to http://localhost:3000/) to verify the generated matrix persists, then check that the text "Sample" is not visible and that the "Tier 1" element is visible.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC007-Restore happens immediately on reload (no loading skeleton shown)
description: Verifies that after a generation, reloading shows the matrix immediately without any loading/skeleton state.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type 'Nimbus CRM' into the Product Name field (index 2).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Nimbus CRM')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('A lightweight CRM for small sales teams.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Small business sales reps and sales managers')
        
        # -> Click the 'Generate Content Matrix' button (index 70).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type 'Leads, pipelines, email templates, reporting' into the Core Features field (index 5).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[4]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Leads, pipelines, email templates, reporting')
        
        # -> Click the Generate Content Matrix button (index 70), then reload the page and verify the 'Content Matrix' is visible immediately without any loading/skeleton state.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Fill the Product fields with the provided values and click 'Generate Content Matrix' to produce the customized matrix.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Forge DevTools')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Developer tooling to speed up CI troubleshooting.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('DevOps engineers and backend developers')
        
        # -> Click the 'Generate Content Matrix' button to produce the customized matrix, wait for the page to update, then verify that the matrix cards show actual text content (check that 'Tier 1 — Narrative' is visible and that 'Forge DevTools' appears in the generated content).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Fill the Core Features field with 'Build logs, flaky test detection, failure grouping' and click 'Generate Content Matrix' so the app can produce a customized content matrix.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[4]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Build logs, flaky test detection, failure grouping')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC010-Generating a second time overwrites persisted matrix and restores the latest on reload
description: Verifies that the latest successful generation replaces the previously persisted matrix and is the one restored after reload.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type 'Alpha Notes' into Product Name (input index 4) and continue with the form fill and generation steps.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Alpha Notes')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('A team note-taking app for project documentation.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Engineering teams and project managers')
        
        # -> Type core features into index 7, generate once, change Product Name to 'Beta Notes', generate again, then reload the page to verify 'Beta Notes' is restored.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[4]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Markdown, search, templates, permissions')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Beta Notes')
        
        # -> Click the 'Generate Content Matrix' button (index 72) to run the second generation using the current Product Name 'Beta Notes'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Wait for the current generation to finish, reload the page, then verify that 'Beta Notes' is visible in the restored content (confirming the latest generation persisted).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC011-No sample banner after reload when a persisted matrix exists
description: Verifies that once a matrix has been generated and persisted, the sample banner does not appear on subsequent visits.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Fill the four form fields with the provided content, click Generate Content Matrix, wait for result, then reload the page to verify the sample banner text 'Sample' is no longer visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Cedar HR')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('HR onboarding workflows for distributed teams.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('HR managers and team leads')
        
        # -> Fill the Core Features field, click Generate Content Matrix, wait for the result, then navigate to '/' (reload) and verify the text 'Sample' is NOT visible on the page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/div[4]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Onboarding checklists, doc collection, task assignments')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Navigate to '/' (reload the page) and verify that the text 'Sample' is NOT visible on the page. If the feature to persist matrices does not exist or the sample banner still appears after reload, report the issue and finish.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
